title: Step-budget streaming training — scale optimization 16x (600 -> ~9,600 gradient steps) via fresh per-epoch resampling, multi-pass inner loop, and cosine LR decay
hypothesis: All six prior experiments ran an identical ~600-gradient-step budget (8 epochs x 75 batches, ~46 s/area, most of it crop extraction), so every negative result — including exp 5's grid-classification head and exp 6's data-diversity increase — is confounded by drastic under-optimization: 600 Adam steps cannot move a 300k-param network out of the center-guess minimum on a memorization task over ~45k positions x 6 lightings, regardless of head or data. The center-collapse at ~2968 m is the signature of an under-trained model, not (yet) evidence about architecture, loss family, or data volume. Scaling the step budget ~16x while holding architecture, loss, batch size, and peak LR fixed directly tests this.
method: Rewrite model/train.py's training procedure only (model/model.py untouched): each epoch draws a FRESH sample of 1,600 crops/bucket (9,600 crops, new positions and rotations) and runs 8 shuffled passes over it, giving 8 epochs x 8 passes x 150 batches = ~9,600 steps and up to ~76,800 distinct crops seen; add cosine LR decay 1e-3 -> 1e-5 over the full step budget (standard for long runs, prevents late-run instability at constant Adam LR); hold training tensors as uint8 and normalize per batch on device so the larger per-epoch sample fits memory. CLI contract (--area/--out-dir/--epochs/--seed) and ONNX export contract unchanged.
expected_outcome: If under-training is the binding constraint, worst-case median error drops materially below the 2968.56 m best — predicted 1,000-2,500 m as the model starts genuinely separating positions. If the metric stays ~2,900-3,100 m despite 16x steps, under-optimization is RULED OUT as the binding constraint, cleanly redirecting future iterations to architecture (the GAP bag-of-features head discards spatial arrangement) or loss family — and giving them an unconfounded training budget to be measured under. Cost: ~2.5-3 min/area vs 46 s, well within harness tolerance.
